Title: Daniel Nadler self-funds OpenEvidence
Description: Nadler invests ~$10M of his own money after selling Kensho to S&P Global for $550M in 2018.

Title: OpenEvidence founded
Description: Daniel Nadler and Zachary Ziegler found OpenEvidence to organize and expand the world's collective medical knowledge using AI.

Title: Jim Breyer / Breyer Capital invests
Description: Jim Breyer (early Facebook backer) and Ken Moelis become first outside investors.

Title: AI scores 90% on USMLE
Description: OpenEvidence's AI model achieves 90 percent score on the United States Medical Licensing Examination.

Title: Mayo Clinic accelerator
Description: OpenEvidence participates in the Mayo Clinic health-technology accelerator program.

Title: 358K monthly physician consultations
Description: 358000 verified U.S. physician consultations per month.

Title: $7.9M annualized revenue
Description: Sacra estimates OpenEvidence at $7.9M in annualized revenue.

Title: ~3M monthly consultations
Description: Approximately 3 million clinical consultations per month.

Title: Series A: $75M at $1B valuation + NEJM partnership
Description: Sequoia Capital leads $75M Series A round valuing OpenEvidence at $1 billion. NEJM content partnership announced alongside.

Title: NEJM partnership announced
Description: Multi-year content agreement with NEJM Group. All content from 1990 forward from NEJM NEJM Evidence NEJM AI NEJM Catalyst and NEJM Journal Watch.

Title: OpenEvidence sues Pathway Medical
Description: OpenEvidence files lawsuit against Canadian AI startup Pathway Medical for prompt injection attacks to access proprietary system prompts.

Title: Forbes AI 50 list
Description: OpenEvidence featured in the Forbes AI 50 list.

Title: Daniel Nadler named TIME100 Health
Description: Daniel Nadler named to the TIME100 Health list of 100 most influential people in global health.

Title: NBC News profile
Description: NBC News publishes major profile: OpenEvidence - Most physicians quietly use this medical AI tool.

Title: NBC News Feature: 650K physicians 27M monthly consultations
Description: NBC News reports 650000 US physicians use OpenEvidence and 27 million clinical consultations per month.

Title: Voice Mode launched
Description: OpenEvidence launches Voice Mode first native speech-to-speech medical AI interface alongside partnership with Cedars-Sinai.

Title: SNO and OpenEvidence Launch Official Generative AI Partnership
Description: Society for Neuro-Oncology designates OpenEvidence as official generative AI partner expanding access to neuro-oncology evidence for 3000+ members in 60 countries.

Title: JOMI Partnership — Peer-Reviewed Surgical Videos
Description: OpenEvidence and Journal of Medical Insight announce first video evidence collaboration bringing 357 peer-reviewed surgical videos onto the platform.

Title: $50M annualized revenue
Description: Sacra estimates OpenEvidence at $50M in annualized revenue growing 30% month-over-month.

Title: JAMA Network partnership
Description: Multi-year content agreement with JAMA Network covering JAMA and 11 specialty journals.

Title: Cease-and-desist to Doximity
Description: OpenEvidence sends cease-and-desist letter to Doximity regarding impersonation and prompt injection attacks.

Title: Pathway files motion to dismiss
Description: Pathway Medical files motion to dismiss OpenEvidence's lawsuit.

Title: OpenEvidence sues Doximity
Description: OpenEvidence files federal lawsuit against Doximity alleging corporate espionage including impersonation of physicians and prompt injection attacks. Case No. 1:25-cv-11802.

Title: 430K physicians / 8.5M consultations/month
Description: 430000+ registered U.S. physicians (40%+ of U.S. physicians) and 8.5 million monthly consultations. 10000+ hospitals.

Title: Series B: $210M at $3.5B valuation + DeepConsult launch
Description: GV (Google Ventures) and Kleiner Perkins co-lead $210M Series B. Nadler owns ~60% net worth $2.3B. Ziegler owns ~10%.

Title: DeepConsult launched
Description: First AI agent purpose-built for physicians. Uses reasoning models to cross-reference hundreds of studies in parallel. 100x+ compute of standard search. Free to all verified U.S. clinicians.

Title: Doximity acquires Pathway Medical for $63M
Description: Doximity acquires Pathway Medical the Canadian AI startup OpenEvidence had sued.

Title: AI scores 100% on USMLE
Description: First AI to achieve perfect 100 percent score on the USMLE.

Title: Visits (AI Scribe) launched
Description: OpenEvidence Visits launched as real-time medical intelligence feature for patient visits. Transcribes encounters generates notes and enriches assessment and plan with latest evidence. Free for all verified US Healthcare Professionals.

Title: OpenEvidence acquires Amaro
Description: Acquires GV-backed AI-native advertising startup founded by Kush and Sauren Khosla. Strengthens ad-monetization infrastructure.

Title: Doximity moves to dismiss
Description: Doximity and two executives move to dismiss OpenEvidence's lawsuit in District of Massachusetts.

Title: Series C: $200M at $6B valuation
Description: GV leads. Sequoia Kleiner Perkins Blackstone Thrive Coatue Bond Craft participate. Nadler net worth rises to $3.6B. 16.5M consultations/month.

Title: $150M annualized revenue
Description: Sacra estimates $150M annualized revenue - 20x YoY growth and 90% gross margins.

Title: NCCN partnership announced
Description: Licensing agreement to make NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology accessible through OpenEvidence.

Title: OpenEvidence moves to dismiss Doximity counterclaims
Description: OpenEvidence calls Doximity's harassment and defamation counterclaims frivolous.

Title: 760K physicians / 18M consultations/month
Description: 760000 registered U.S. physicians and approximately 18 million clinical consultations per month.

Title: ACEP partnership
Description: American College of Emergency Physicians partnership announced.

Title: Doctor Dialer launched (limited release)
Description: HIPAA-secure dialer for doctor-patient telemedicine calls combined with Visits suite. 37M minutes of doctor-patient communications by Feb 2026.

Title: ChatGPT Health (OpenAI competitor)
Description: OpenAI launches consumer-facing ChatGPT Health. 260+ physicians 60 countries 600K feedback interactions.

Title: ChatGPT for Healthcare (OpenAI enterprise)
Description: OpenAI launches enterprise HIPAA-compliant AI tool for hospitals and health systems.

Title: Series D: $250M at $12B valuation
Description: Thrive Capital and DST Global co-lead. Nadler net worth $7.6B. Ziegler ~7.3% worth $875M. 95% of new users from physician word-of-mouth.

Title: Clinical Trial Matching launched
Description: Automatically surfaces active and recruiting clinical trials and evaluates each against patient clinical context. Matching disease treatment history and eligibility criteria. Location filtering and distance display.

Title: Sutter Health deployment
Description: Embedded into Epic EHR workflows for Sutter Health physicians across California. First health system deployment.

Title: Doctor Dialer wide release + Messaging/Fax/Voicemail
Description: Dialer wide release with new messaging faxing and voicemail features. 37M minutes already powered.

Title: Vercel blog case study
Description: Reveals Next.js frontend on Vercel with Fluid Compute. <5% of infra spend. 90% serverless cost reduction.

Title: Wiley + Cochrane partnership
Description: Wiley and OpenEvidence partner. Includes Cochrane Systematic Reviews content.

Title: 1M consultations in single day
Description: First time in history one million clinical consultations between NPI verified physicians and an AI system in 24 hours.

Title: NORD partnership
Description: Partnership to expand access to rare disease information for clinicians patients and families worldwide.

Title: AAO-HNS partnership
Description: First-of-its-kind partnership to keep clinical practice guidelines aligned with latest evidence.

Title: Coding Intelligence launched
Description: Automates medical coding - ICD-10 E/M and CPT suggestions inline. RVU values. Medicare MPPR optimization.

Title: Mount Sinai deployment
Description: Enterprise-wide deployment across 7 hospitals in Epic EHR. First to extend to nurses and pharmacists.

Title: Dotflows launched
Description: AI-native customization - reusable natural language prompts. Community library. Type '.' to invoke.

Title: Abridge NEJM/JAMA partnership (competitor)
Description: Abridge announces evidence integration with same journal partners as OpenEvidence - direct convergence.

Title: Wiley Neurology Expansion
Description: Clinical findings from journals of four leading neurological societies added.

Title: ChatGPT for Clinicians (OpenAI competitor)
Description: OpenAI launches free clinical AI for verified US physicians. GPT-5.4. CME credits. HealthBench Professional benchmark.

Title: NCCN Treatment Algorithms integrated
Description: NCCN Guidelines Treatment Algorithms integrated into OpenEvidence at point of care.

Title: Cedars-Sinai deployment
Description: Third health system. Patient-profile-tailored queries from EHR. Access for physicians nurses pharmacists therapists.

Title: ACOG partnership
Description: Strategic collaboration for ob-gyn care and womens health.

Title: GINA partnership
Description: Asthma strategy reports and clinical recommendations available through OpenEvidence.

Title: SSO partnership
Description: Strategic partnership for surgical oncology evidence. Launch of SSO Innovator Grant.

Title: Doximity + Aledade partnership
Description: Doximity integrates Clinical AI Suite into Aledade Assist for value-based care.

Title: AAO partnership
Description: American Academy of Ophthalmology Preferred Practice Patterns integrated.

Title: Elsevier ClinicalKey AI partnership
Description: Elsevier Health partners with OpenEvidence to develop ClinicalKey AI. Content from The Lancet Cell Grays Anatomy and specialized journals. Early access Nov 2023 general release early 2024.

Title: Tandem partnership (Prescriptions & Prior Auth)
Description: OpenEvidence and Tandem partner to streamline evidence-based prescribing and prior authorizations. Brings prescription generation and prior-auth submission directly within platform.

Title: SNO partnership
Description: Society for Neuro-Oncology and OpenEvidence announce partnership. SSO Innovator Grant launched. Designates OpenEvidence as SNO official Generative AI Partner.

Title: STAT Breakthrough Summit — hospital pitch
Description: CTO Zachary Ziegler presents at STAT Breakthrough Summit West. OpenEvidence pitches hospitals for formal relationships beyond individual physician users. 650K physicians claimed.

Title: ASCO Guidelines integration
Description: OpenEvidence integrates ASCO Guidelines figures and flowcharts into specialty oncology model. Direct links to ASCO source documents.

Title: Pathway Labs EchoNext FDA clearance + OpenEvidence partnership
Description: Pathway Labs announces FDA clearance for EchoNext — worlds first multicondition AI for cardiology. Partnership with OpenEvidence to make EchoNext available to 500K+ physicians. Nature Medicine publishes world-first heart transplant due to AI detection.

Title: OpenEvidence no longer available in EU
Description: Mushtaq Bilal PhD posts on X that OpenEvidence is no longer available in the EU due to EU regulation. Spanish doctor La Traumatóloga Geek also posts about the departure.

Title: Nature Medicine publishes world-first AI heart transplant case
Description: Nature Medicine publishes peer-reviewed case where EchoNext detected undiagnosed heart failure leading to worlds first heart transplant due to AI detection of disease.

Title: NYT — Doctors Thought It Was Asthma AI Flagged Heart Problem
Description: New York Times publishes major article on EchoNext AI detecting structural heart disease from ECGs that doctors missed. Features patient story and Dr Pierre Elias at NewYork-Presbyterian.

Title: First FDA-cleared AI tool integrated into OpenEvidence
Description: Travis Zack CMO of OpenEvidence confirms EchoNext is the first FDA-cleared AI tool available within OpenEvidence. Free to any doctor using the platform. First time OpenEvidence offers an AI diagnostic tool.

Title: Jim Breyer posts about Pathway Labs on X
Description: Jim Breyer (Breyer Capital) posts on X about leading Pathway Labs first institutional financing and partnership with OpenEvidence. Notes EchoNext detects disease invisible on routine ECG.

Title: STAT tweets EchoNext FDA clearance
Description: STAT News tweets about EchoNext FDA clearance and OpenEvidence integration. Sweeping new AI to detect heart conditions.

Title: Arfur Rock on X — AI ads cross the rubicon
Description: Notable X post analyzing OpenEvidence ad model: $150M RR on just ads +3x from August on under 1M users 90% gross margins. Predicts 2026 will be the year AI ads cross the rubicon.

Title: New PubMed — OpenEvidence EU geoblocking lessons
Description: Lancet Regional Health Europe publishes letter on regulatory certainty lessons from OpenEvidence geoblocking in EU and UK over EU AI Act.

Title: New PubMed — OpenEvidence assessed against AAOS ACL guidelines
Description: J ISAKOS publishes evaluation of OpenEvidence responses to American Academy of Orthopaedic Surgeons clinical practice guidelines for ACL reconstruction.

Sub_Category: Abridge Keynote — Patient-Centered Clinician Intelligence Platform
Title: Abridge unveils first AI-native clinician intelligence platform at NYC Keynote. Partners with Nvidia for foundation model. Eli Lilly investment. Northwestern Medicine rollout. Beyond AI scribe.
Description: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260611779141/en/Abridge-Unveils-Patient-Centered-Clinician-Intelligence-Platform-Connecting-Care-Delivery-Payment-and-Evidence-Based-Treatment

Title: Nature Medicine publishes frontier LLM vs clinical AI benchmarking study
Description: Nature Medicine publishes study showing GPT-5.2 Gemini 3.1 Pro and Claude Opus 4.6 outperform OpenEvidence and UpToDate Expert AI across MedQA HealthBench and real clinical queries. OpenEvidence scores lowest on clarity.

Title: Beckers covers Nature Medicine benchmarking study
Description: Beckers Hospital Review covers the study. On MedQA Gemini scored 97.4% vs OpenEvidence 89.6%. UpToDate AI refused 19% of queries. OpenEvidence racked up 52 flagged low-scoring responses.

Title: New PubMed — OpenEvidence evaluated in OB/GYN clinical scenarios
Description: Applied Clinical Informatics publishes comparison of ChatGPT Gemini and OpenEvidence in obstetric and gynecologic clinical decision scenarios.

Title: OpenEvidence demands Nature Medicine retraction
Description: OpenEvidence posts LinkedIn response alleging undisclosed conflicts of interest and irredeemable methodological flaws. Nature Medicine declines to address retraction demand. FixHealth.ai and ASCO AI in Oncology analyze controversy.

Title: New PubMed — LLM comparison for clinical pharmacy cases
Description: JACCP publishes comparison of LLM-based systems and prompt engineering for internal medicine clinical pharmacy cases.

Title: New PubMed — LLM risk framework for healthcare
Description: BMJ Mental Health publishes framework for estimating risk of LLMs in healthcare using simulation.

Title: UpDoc receives FDA clearance for clinical AI platform
Description: UpDoc debuts first FDA-cleared agentic clinical AI platform for real-time patient care delivery. Chronic disease management at scale. Deployed at four leading health systems.

Title: Nature Medicine Study: General-Purpose LLMs Outperform Specialized Clinical AI
Description: A study published in Nature Medicine found GPT-5.2 Gemini 3.1 Pro and Claude Opus 4.6 outperformed OpenEvidence and UpToDate Expert AI on MedQA HealthBench and real clinical queries. OpenEvidence disputed the findings on X/Twitter.

Title: Coding Intelligence launched (Live in Visits)
Description: OpenEvidence launched Coding Intelligence to automate medical coding generate MDM breakdowns and capture missing reimbursement. Live in Visits.
